outputs.description: The cracked, plaintext version of the password (where the password is crackable). SpyCloud.CompassApplicationData.password_type Password type for original password as found in the data breach. This will either be plaintext or one of the many password hash/encryption types (SHA1, MD5, 3DES, etc).
outputs.name: SpyCloud.CompassApplicationData.password_plaintext

outputs.description: URL extracted from Botnet data. This is the URL that is captured from a key logger installed on an infected user's system. SpyCloud.CompassApplicationData.user_browser Browser name.
outputs.name: SpyCloud.CompassApplicationData.target_url

outputs.description: List of one or more IP addresses in alphanumeric format. Both IPV4 and IPv6 addresses are supported. SpyCloud.CompassApplicationData.infected_machine_id A unique identifier either extracted from an infostealer log, when present, or an RFC 4122-compliant universally unique identifier (UUID) generated by SpyCloud, when no identifier is present in an infected record. The method of generation of these identifiers varies by malware family and may or may not conform to a UUID format. For the ID's in t
outputs.name: SpyCloud.CompassApplicationData.ip_addresses

outputs.description: The local path to the malicious software installed on the infected user's system. SpyCloud.CompassApplicationData.infected_time Date
outputs.name: SpyCloud.CompassApplicationData.infected_path

outputs.description: SpyCloud.CompassApplicationData.user_sys_domain System domain. This usually comes from Botnet data. SpyCloud.CompassApplicationData.user_hostname
outputs.name: The time at which the user's system was infected with malicious software.

outputs.description: System hostname. This usually comes from Botnet data. SpyCloud.CompassApplicationData.user_os System OS name. This usually comes from Botnet data.
outputs.name: String

outputs.description: System registered owner name. This usually comes from Botnet data. SpyCloud.CompassApplicationData.source_id Numerical breach ID. This correlates directly with the id field in Breach Catalog objects.
outputs.name: SpyCloud.CompassApplicationData.user_sys_registered_owner

outputs.description: Date The date on which we ingested the breached data into our systems. This is the same date on which the data becomes publicly available to our customers. SpyCloud.CompassApplicationData.target_domain
outputs.name: SpyCloud.CompassApplicationData.spycloud_publish_date

outputs.description: SLD extracted from 'target_url' field. SpyCloud.CompassApplicationData.target_subdomain Subdomain and SLD extracted from 'target_url' field.
outputs.name: String

outputs.description: Severity is a numeric code representing severity of a breach record. This can be used in API requests to ensure only Breach Records with plaintext password are returned. Possible values are: 2 -> Email only severity. This record is part of an email-only list. 5 -> Informational severity. This severity value is given to breach records where we have a non-crackable password hash, or no password at all. 20 -> High severity. This severity value is given to breach records where we have an email addre
outputs.name: SpyCloud.CompassApplicationData.severity

outputs.description: Email address. SpyCloud.CompassApplicationData.email_domain Domain extracted from 'email_address' field. This is not a SLD, but everything after the '@' symbol.
outputs.name: SpyCloud.CompassApplicationData.email

outputs.description: Username extracted from 'email' field. This is everything before the '@' symbol. SpyCloud.CompassApplicationData.domain Domain name.
outputs.name: SpyCloud.CompassApplicationData.email_username
